description:
Gate the (now correctness-only) epic closed. Blocked by CF1 (mutagen-33b.1) and CF2a (mutagen-33b.2). CF2b (mutagen-33b.3) is DEFERRED and intentionally NOT a blocker.

SCOPE CHANGE (2026-06-22 review): F2 (coverage-driven test selection) was split into epic mutagen-xez, so this capstone NO LONGER blocks on / verifies F2b parity. mutagen-33b is now the parallel-path CORRECTNESS workstream only.

## Verify
- CF1 worker-crash test green and NON-VACUOUS (recording code_server stub on a snapshotted module); no mutated module survives an aborted parallel run; the restore sweep is ordered strictly after sibling-task teardown.
- CF2a experimental warning present + documented (help text + README), fires on the resolved max_concurrency > 1, silent for 1.
- Full suite green incl. e2e / downstream_integration; a parallel (--max-concurrency 2) e2e run completes without leaving global :cover / code-server / ExUnit state dirty.

## Honest closed-state (review finding H5)
On close, parallel-path correctness is GATED + WARNED, not fully achieved -- the real in-VM-vs-OS isolation fix (CF2b, mutagen-33b.3) remains deferred behind a measured-need build-gate. The epic title/summary reflect "gate + warn parallel; defer real isolation" so "Done" is not mis-read as "parallel is now safe".

## Done
- CF1 and CF2a closed and the above checks pass on the epic branch.
title: Capstone: parallel-path correctness verified (CF1 restore + CF2a gate)
